rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Substitute Patrick Mcnerney As Does 1 And 101 And Veritas Investments Inc As Does 2 And 102
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM's Motion For Leave To Substitute Patrick Mcnerney As Does 1 And 101 And Veritas Investments Inc As Does 2 And 102 Plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association's motion for leave to substitute Patrick McNerney as Does 1 and 101 and Veritas Investments, Inc, as Does 2 and 102 is denied without prejudice to being refiled as an ex parte application if within the next two months the trial date is continued to a day on or after May 6, 2019. Any legal prejudice to Mr. McNerney and Veritas by the granting of the motion is eliminated if the trial is continued to May or June 2019 as Mint Collection expects will happen, but has not yet occurred nor is there a pending motion to continue the trial. Absent a trial continuance, the prejudice to Mr. McNerney and Veritas from having to prepare for a trial two months away and being deprived of the opportunity to file a summary judgment motion requires denial of the motion. If the trial is continued, granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage in a case. Mint Collection's alter ego allegations, even if not legally sufficient, do not show that Mint Collection will be unable to viably allege alter ego liability against Mr. McNerney and Veritas. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mint Collection is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC.,, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. MBC-related defendants' demurrer to the entirety of the second amended complaint and to the first, eighth and ninth causes of action therein is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the entirety of the SAC; b) sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for violation of residential building standards and the eighth cause of action for unlawful LLC distributions; and c) overruled as to the ninth cause of action for breach of contract. Because there are four different MBC-related parties, each with what appears to be a different role regarding the real property that is the subject of this case, as to each cause of action plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association should allege why it contends each of these defendants is liable instead of lumping them all together in a collectively defined term. Notwithstanding that it was given another opportunity to allege viable clams for violation of the residential building standards and unlawful LLC distributions, Mint Collection has not done and it now appears that it is unable to do so. The second amended complaint does not disclose on its face that all or any portion of the breach of contract claim is necessarily time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the MBC-related defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC.,, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. MBC-related defendants motion to strike the entirety and specific portions of the second amended complaint is denied in its entirety. While the better course would have been either not to re-plead the strict liability and breach of fiduciary duty claims or to explicitly state that they were re-plead for appellate preservation purposes only, because plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association does not maintain that those claims remain viable in this court, there is no reason to strike them. Mint Collection's pleading of alter ego allegations is not impermissible in the way it did so. At this point in the case the allegations and prayer seeking attorney's fees, expert fees and costs is largely immaterial and the court cannot say with certitude that Mint Collection will not be entitled to attorney's fees, expert fees and costs based on post-judgment motions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mint Collection is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KARA L. ESBORG VS. COMCAST CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Issued July 11, 2018 To O.C. Communications
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 4, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT COMCAST OF CALIFORNIA III, INC.'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Issued July 11, 2018 To O.C. Communications. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Comcast Corp.'s ("Comcast") Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoena for Production of Business Records is GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. No request for monetary sanctions has been made and thus monetary sanctions against any party is DENIED AS UNSOUGHT. All other sanctions, if any, are DENIED. As the prevailing party, Comcast shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating this tentative ruling, and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. SIMONE V ECHEGUREN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT SIMONE ECHEGUREN's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant Simone Echeguren's motion to set aside the default entered against her on January 16, 2018 and the default judgment entered against her on January 18, 2018 is granted. The default and default judgment are now set aside. Ms. Echeguren must file an answer no later September 13, 2018. Ms. Echeguren has shown that she is entitled to have the default and default judgment set aside per CCP 473.5. Plaintiff LVNV Funding has not shown that Ms. Echeguren's sworn statements that she first learned of this case on or around January 18, 2018 and did not seek to evade service are false. California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and the absence of any claimed prejudice by LVNV further supports the granting of the motion. While this motion could have been filed earlier, the failure to do so should not deprive Ms. Echeguren of her right to defend the case on its merits. LVNV's request for fees is denied since LVNV should have stipulated to the setting aside of the default and default judgment which would have made this motion unnecessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Echeguren is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: OREGON MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BRUCE I. GILBERT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sunset Scavenger Company'S Motion For Protective Order.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 4, 2018, Line 3, Defendant Sunset Scavenger Company'S Motion For Protective Order. (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Sunset Scavenger Company's ("Sunset's") Motion for Protective Order is DENIED. No request for monetary sanctions has been made and thus monetary sanctions against any party is DENIED AS UNSOUGHT. All other sanctions, if any, are DENIED. As the prevailing party Oregon Mutual Insurance Co. ("Oregon Mutual") shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating this tentative ruling, and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. "The court, for good cause shown," may issue a "protective order." Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2031.060(b). "[T]he issuance and formulation of protective orders are to a large extent discretionary." Nativi v. Deutsche Bank Nat'l Trust Co., 223 Cal. App. 4th 261, 316-17 (2014); seeRaymond Handling Concepts Corp. v. Superior Court, 39 Cal. App. 4th 584, 588 (1995). The state has two substantial interests in regulating pretrial discovery. The first is to facilitate the search for truth and promote justice. The second is to protect the legitimate privacy interests of the litigants and third parties. The interest in truth and justice is promoted by allowing liberal discovery of information in the possession of the opposing party. The interest in privacy is promoted by restricting the procurement or dissemination of information from the opposing party upon a showing of "good cause." (end of tentative ruling part 1 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: OREGON MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BRUCE I. GILBERT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sunset Scavenger Company'S Motion For Protective Order. (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 4, 2018, Line 3, Defendant Sunset Scavenger Company'S Motion For Protective Order. (For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2) Stadish v. Superior Court, 71 Cal. App. 4th 1130, 1145 (1999) (citations and internal quotation marks omitted); see Nativi, 223 Cal. App. 4that 317 ("A trial court must balance the various interests in deciding whether dissemination of the documents should be restricted."). "[T]he burden is on the party seeking the protective order to show good cause for whatever order is sought." Fairmont Ins. Co. v. Superior Court, 22 Cal. 4th 245, 255 (2000). But the movant cannot satisfy this burden by submitting "conclusory" declarations "lack[ing] any factual specificity."Nativi, 223 Cal. App. 4th at 318. It is error for the trial court to issue an umbrella protective order limiting or quashing the production of documents when the moving party fails to make a "factual showing that (1) the documents . . . contained confidential commercial information or information in which it had any protectable interest or (2) dissemination of the documents to the public would result in injury." Id. Here Sunset argues that a protective order should issue continuing a scheduled deposition of its PMK and limiting production of documents at this deposition. Sunset's principal argument is that it expects to prevail on Demurrer which would resolve the case and moot discovery. But the Court takes judicial notice Judge Charles Haines minute order August 30, 2018 wherein Sunset's demurrer to the Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") is overruled and wherein Sunset is ordered to answer within 21 days. Presently the action is not going to be dismissed and there is no good cause to delay discovery any longer. Although no clear rationale is articulated in its memorandum, Sunset argues in declaration that the scope of the PMK document production should be limited to avoid "unwarranted annoyance, undue burden, and undue cost." Hagopian Decl. 11. But Sunset offers no reasons in support of these conclusions. Mere conclusions, alone "lack[ing] any factual specificity" cannot support a finding of good cause.Nativi, 223 Cal. App. 4th at 318. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MERRILEE HARRIS VS. COLINDRES & ASSOCIATES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF MERRILEE HARRIS' Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Merrilee Harris' motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted in part and defendant Colindres & Associates, Inc.'s request for a trial continuance is denied. Ms. Harris' proposed new claims (the abuse of process, injunction and a portion of her declaratory relief claims) alleging invalidity of the mechanic's lien and lis pendens are not actionable and thus she is denied leave to amend those claims. The filing of a mechanic's lien and a lis pendens are absolutely privileged. Ms. Harris is granted leave to allege her proposed new section 17200 claim and the portion of her proposed new declaratory relief claim not alleging invalidity of the mechanic's lien and lis pendens because those claims have at least arguable merit and the policy of California is to liberally allow amendments to pleadings at any stage of the case. C&A has not shown that there is a need for a trial continuance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for C&A is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ASIAN INC. VS. ELIZABETH P SOUSA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT VICTOR MARQUEZ' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant Victor Marquez's demurrer to the third cause of action for attempted extortion in the first amended cross-complaint filed by Elizabeth Souza is overruled. Liberally reading the cross-complaint as required by CCP 452, it adequately alleges sufficient ultimate facts to infer Mr. Marquez's specific intent to commit the crime of attempted extortion. As explained in the ruling on Mr. Marquez's anti-SLAPP motion to strike, Ms. Sousa pleads a claim for attempted extortion. rulings.Case Title: ASIAN INC. VS. ELIZABETH P SOUSA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Victor Marquezs Special Anti Slapp Motion And Special Anti Slapp Motion To Strike Defendant And Cross Complainant Elizabeth P Sousas First Amended Cross Complaint.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 8, Cross Defendant Victor Marquezs Special Anti Slapp Motion And Special Anti Slapp Motion To Strike Defendant And Cross Complainant Elizabeth P Sousas First Amended Cross Complaint. Cross-defendant Victor Marquez's anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike the third cause of action for attempted extortion in the first amended cross-complaint filed by Elizabeth Sousa is denied. The merits of the motion will be addressed even though the motion was filed outside the 60-day time period of CCP 425.16(f). Mr. Marquez received an extension of time to file his responsive pleading and reasonably believed that he could still file an anti-SLAPP motion. Mr. Marquez's September 11, 2017 letter constitutes attempted criminal extortion as a matter of law and thus the letter does not constitute protected activity per CCP 425.16. The letter states that Asian, Inc. is willing to fulfill all payment obligations if Ms. Sousa turned over the art and provided an accounting. The letter further states that: "If Ms. Sousa does not agree to these conditions, we will have no choice but to report her to the City Attorney's office and the District Attorney's office for the charge of larceny." The letter threatens to report Ms. Sousa to the authorities unless she relinquishes the artwork. It is therefore attempted extortion. (Stenehjem v. Sareen (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1405, 1417-1418.) Mr. Marquez makes much of Ms. Sousa's miscitation of the September 27, 2017 e-mail and his subsequent communications that purportedly show that he had no intent to threaten Ms. Sousa. The e-mail did not clearly unequivocally rescind the prior threat. Moreover, Mr. Marquez does not cite any authority holding that his subsequent communications could somehow "wipe the slate clean." Ms. Sousa's request for fees is denied as the anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike was not frivolous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Sousa is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ELECTRICAL INDUSTRY SERVICE BUREAU, INC. VS. THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Travelers Indemnity Company Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 12, Defendant The Travelers Indemnity Company Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Hearing required. The court reaffirms its previously issued tentative ruling from the August 30, 2018 calendar. Defendant The Travelers Indemnity Company's demurrer to both causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Electrical Industry Service Bureau, Inc. is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for failure to pay on a payment bond and overruled as to the second cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law. EISB filed this lawsuit after the expiration of the limitations period for a claim for failure to pay on a payment bond. EISB's effort to be relieved of its untimely first cause of action based on equitable tolling is unavailing. EISB has not alleged that it filed a "claim" prior to filing of this lawsuit. Thus, for instance, in Structural Steel Fabricators, Inc. v. City of Orange (1995) 40 Cal. App. 4th 449, the "first claim" was a prior lawsuit. Liberally construed, the allegations in the FAC suggest that EISB may be seeking to invoke equitable estoppel as a basis to avoid the statute of limitations governing a claim for failure to pay on a payment bond, notwithstanding that EISB does not mention equitable estoppel in either the FAC or in its opposition memorandum. Nonetheless, EISB's allegations fall far short of what is required to adequately plead equitable estoppel. (Lantzy v. Centex Homes (2003) 31 Cal. 4th 363, 385). Citing Krantz v. BT Visual Images, L.L.C. (2001) 89 Cal. App. 4th 164, Travelers's sole argument in its initial memorandum as to the UCL claim is that the UCL claim fails because the payment bond claim failed. Not so. Unlike this case, Krantz did not involve the failure of an "antecedent claim" based on the statute of limitations. Per Business and Professions Code 17208, the UCL has its own statute of limitations and that four year limitations period, not the limitations period of the "antecedent" claim, generally governs the UCL claim. (Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal 4th 163, 178-79 (four year limitations period applies to UCL claim based on a violation of the Labor Code even though the Labor Code had a shorter limitations period)); see also Business and Professions Code 17205 (UCL claims are cumulative of claims under other California laws)). Counsel for Travelers is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TRENT JASON VS. AMERICAN AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION OF NORTHERN
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE, CSAA INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants CSAA Insurance Services, Inc. and CSAA Insurance Exchange's demurrer to the fourth through eighth causes of action for unfair competition, misrepresentation, false promise, deceit, and negligent infliction of emotional distress in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Trent Jason is sustained without leave to amend as to all those causes of action. As for the unfair competition claim, the SAC fails to allege facts showing that CSAA engaged in unfair, dishonest, deceptive or fraudulent practices with intent to defraud the public in violation of Civil Code 1711. As for the misrepresentation, false promise, and deceit claims, the SAC does not allege adequate facts showing who made what misrepresentations or false promises, and when, how and to whom such misrepresentations or false promises were made. The SAC also fails to plead facts that show that Mr. Jason actually and justifiably relied on CSAA's alleged misrepresentation or false promise to his detriment and as a result, incurred actual damages. The NIED claim fails because bad faith, not negligence, is the sole remedy against insurers like CSAA for damages arising out of allegedly mishandled claims. After being given an opportunity to allege viable claims, it appears that Mr. Jason is not able to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for CSAA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: DARREN MANFRO VS. DANYI YU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Stay The Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 4, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF DARREN MANFRO 's Motion To Stay The Proceedings. Plaintiff Darren Manfro's motion to stay this case for one year due to Mr. Manfro's current incarceration is denied without prejudice to being refiled based on a fuller showing of need. Mr. Manfro fails to provide even the most basic information supporting his stay request. There is no information regarding where Mr. Manfro is incarcerated or the status of his criminal case. No trial date has been set in this case and, based on the minimal information about the charges against him, it is entirely possible that Mr. Manfro could be released from custody well before trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
